transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: It is late spring in Bellwether: mild, changeable, with cool mornings and occasional showers.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: The bus leaves you at Bellwether just after nine. Its engine noise thins along the road until the village is quiet again.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: For now, there is the road ahead, your suitcase at your feet, and Eleanor's key in your pocket.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You stop for a while and listen. Bellwether is quiet, but never entirely still.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You wait beneath the shelter. Traffic is sparse enough that every engine can be heard before it appears.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You stop for a while and listen. Bellwether is quiet, but never entirely still.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You slow down and pay attention to the place rather than merely passing through it.

transcript.speaker: Notebook
transcript.text: The road has a rhythm: Long quiet gaps separate the traffic; regular vehicles are easy to distinguish from unfamiliar ones.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You make your way to Village Road.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: The road is busy with passing traffic.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You pick up a few ordinary household necessities. It is a small thing, but carrying them back makes your stay feel less temporary.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Mrs Ellis is taking a short pause between errands.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Route 7 bus is nearby, approaching Bellwether on the return service.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You linger without a destination and watch Bellwether carry on around you.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Mrs Ellis is taking a short pause between errands.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Route 7 bus is nearby, resuming the route through Bellwether.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You make your way to Village Shop.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You stop for a while and listen. Bellwether is quiet, but never entirely still.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You read the local pages over twice. Parish notices and small advertisements reveal more about the surrounding villages than the headlines do.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You browse slowly enough to notice which shelves are practical, which are hopeful, and which things the shopkeeper expects everyone to need.

transcript.speaker: Notebook
transcript.text: You review the last few things you thought worth remembering.

transcript.speaker: Notebook
transcript.text: The road has a rhythm — Long quiet gaps separate the traffic; regular vehicles are easy to distinguish from unfamiliar ones.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You make your way to Village Green.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: There are 1 familiar villager moving around the green.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Mrs Ellis is resting for a few minutes on the village green.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You stop for a while and listen. Bellwether is quiet, but never entirely still.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: An old oak shades a bench and noticeboard. Paths lead toward the road, the cottage lane, the church and the lower meadow.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: The village has grown busier around the green and shops. The road is quiet between scheduled buses. Blackbirds work through the wet grass.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: There are 1 familiar villager moving around the green.

transcript.label: Walk down to the riverside path

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Mrs Ellis is reading the notices posted near the green.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You make your way to Riverside Path.

transcript.label: Watch the Water for a While

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: The river looks slow until you fix your attention on leaves and foam moving beneath the footbridge.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You follow the river beneath alder branches, learning where the bank is firm and where the meadow path disappears after rain.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: The weather has shifted: light rain, 15°C, with gentle wind.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You stop for a while and listen. Bellwether is quiet, but never entirely still.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You slow down and pay attention to the place rather than merely passing through it.

transcript.speaker: Notebook
transcript.text: Old water lines: Debris caught above the present bank shows how far the river can rise after sustained rain.

transcript.label: Climb back to the village green

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You make your way to Village Green.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: For the moment, the green has fallen quiet.

transcript.label: Take a Slow Walk Around the Green

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You follow the worn paths around the green, learning which gate sticks and where the ground stays damp.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: An old oak shades a bench and noticeboard. Paths lead toward the road, the cottage lane, the church and the lower meadow.

transcript.text: The village has grown busier around the green and shops. The road is quiet between scheduled buses. Blackbirds work through the wet grass.

transcript.text: For the moment, the green has fallen quiet.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You make your way to Ashcroft Cottage.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Freshly disturbed soil and a growing cleared patch show that someone has been working in the garden.

transcript.speaker: Journal
transcript.text: Main Story completed: A House Left Waiting.

transcript.speaker: Journal
transcript.text: Main Story added: In Eleanor's Hand.

transcript.speaker: Eleanor's Letter
transcript.text: If you are reading this, then you came. Put the kettle on before you decide anything important. The house is kinder when it is warm.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: Below the signature, in different ink, is a final line: Do not worry if the bells wake you.

transcript.speaker: Journal
transcript.text: Main Story completed: In Eleanor's Hand.

transcript.speaker: Journal
transcript.text: Side Story added: The Cottage Garden.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Mara is gathering garden cuttings into a compost pile.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You stop for a while and listen. Bellwether is quiet, but never entirely still.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You choose a book from Eleanor's shelves and read until the sounds outside become part of the room.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Mara is making notes about restoring the cottage garden.

transcript.speaker: Notebook
transcript.text: You review the last few things you thought worth remembering.

transcript.speaker: Notebook
transcript.text: The road has a rhythm — Long quiet gaps separate the traffic; regular vehicles are easy to distinguish from unfamiliar ones.

transcript.text: You make your way to Village Green.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: For the moment, the green has fallen quiet.

transcript.label: Sit on the Bench and Watch the Green

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You sit beneath the old oak. People cross the green on errands that make sense to them and, slowly, begin to make sense to you.

transcript.speaker: Observation
transcript.text: The green no longer feels like a landmark on a map. You are beginning to recognize its rhythms.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: The worn path you have used across the green seems to meet the road a little farther north than you remember. Looking away and back does not help.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You linger without a destination and watch Bellwether carry on around you.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: The day's movement is beginning to turn homeward.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Jonah is taking a short break on the village green.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You make your way to Ashcroft Cottage.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Freshly disturbed soil and a growing cleared patch show that someone has been working in the garden.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: Dust rises, cloths darken, and one small part of the cottage begins to feel inhabited again.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Mara is working on the sticking garden gate.

transcript.speaker: Observation
transcript.text: You are beginning to know which floorboards speak and which windows catch the afternoon light.

transcript.label: Check the Post and Front Step

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: There is no letter today. The stone by the step is damp where the morning has not quite dried.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Mara is resting on the cottage step with a cup of tea.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: Night settles over Ashcroft Cottage. When you wake, pale morning light has found the curtains.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Somewhere in the village, a bakery door opens. A bicycle bell rings once on the road.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You slow down and pay attention to the place rather than merely passing through it.

transcript.speaker: Notebook
transcript.text: Patterns in the cottage: Wear on handles, shelves and floorboards suggests a small set of rooms were used far more often than the rest.

transcript.speaker: Notebook
transcript.text: Recent activity at Ashcroft Cottage: Freshly disturbed soil and a growing cleared patch show that someone has been working in the garden.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: The weather has shifted: heavy rain, 11°C, with brisk wind.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Mara is resting on the cottage step with a cup of tea.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: Rain works across the cottage in layers: roof, glass, gutter, leaves. For a while, every sound has an ordinary source.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: From the room you have begun using most, you hear the small settling creak that normally comes from the empty room upstairs.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You make your way to Village Green.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: For the moment, the green has fallen quiet.

transcript.speaker: Journal
transcript.text: Main Story completed: The Morning After.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: The green looks ordinary in the new light. Still, you have the odd feeling that someone was expecting you.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You stop for a while and listen. Bellwether is quiet, but never entirely still.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: An old oak shades a bench and noticeboard. Paths lead toward the road, the cottage lane, the church and the lower meadow.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Bellwether is settling into its morning rhythm. A delivery van passes through the village. Blackbirds work through the wet grass.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: For the moment, the green has fallen quiet.

transcript.label: Follow the path to the churchyard

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Mara is asking a villager what they remember about the cottage garden.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You make your way to St Bartholomew's Churchyard.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: The yews keep the air still. Beyond the wall, Bellwether continues with errands, doors and distant engines.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You walk between stones softened by weather and lichen. Familiar village surnames repeat across generations.

transcript.text: Mrs Ellis is walking quietly through the churchyard.

transcript.text: The weather has shifted: light rain, 11°C, with gentle wind.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Route 7 bus is nearby, approaching Bellwether along the outer road.

transcript.speaker: Observation
transcript.text: You can now place several houses by their gates, gardens, and the sounds that come from behind them.

transcript.speaker: Narrator

transcript.text: You make your way to Bus Stop.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You wait beneath the shelter. Traffic is sparse enough that every engine can be heard before it appears.

transcript.speaker: Observation
transcript.text: The road beyond the bus stop has begun to feel less like the way out and more like one edge of Bellwether.
